blocks.content: Late June. Her porch again. The air thick and slow the way it got when Georgia couldn't decide between afternoon and evening.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Are you nervous about Fourth of July.
He looked at her. She was looking at the yard, not at him.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: We've been building to it a long time.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: The whole summer feels like a buildup.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Whatever happens — we're fine. You know that.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: The fight in October was pretty bad.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: We made it through the fight in October.
The porch light hummed. Somewhere down the block a dog was barking at nothing. He reached over and she leaned her head against his shoulder.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I already know it's going to be okay. That's kind of the thing. I already know.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Then let's stop being nervous and just let it be what it is.
She didn't move her head off his shoulder. He didn't ask her to.
